1630	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1631	///
1632	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1633	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1634	    state: &mut LuaState,
1635	    z: ZIO,
1636	    name: &[u8],
1637	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1638	) -> LuaStatus {
1639	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1640	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1641	    state.inc_nny();
1642	
1643	    let mut p = SParser {
1644	        z,
1645	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1646	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1647	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1648	        name: name.to_vec(),
1649	    };
1650	
1651	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1652	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1653	
1654	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1655	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1656	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1657	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1658	
1659	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1660	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1661	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1662	
1663	    // C: decnny(L)
1664	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1665	    state.dec_nny();
1666	
1667	    status
1668	}
1669

1671	// PORT STATUS
1672	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)

1678	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1679	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1680	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1681	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1682	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1683	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1684	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1685	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,

2722	/// C: LClosure *luaY_parser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, Mbuffer *buff, Dyndata *dyd,
2723	///                           const char *name, int firstchar)
2724	/// Top-level entry point: parses a chunk and returns the main LClosure.
2725	/// LUAI_FUNC visibility.
2726	///
2727	/// PORT NOTE: In C, returns `LClosure *` (a GC object). In Rust (Phase A),
2728	///   we return `Box<LuaProto>` since we don't have GcRef<LuaLClosure> ready.
2729	///   Phase B will wrap this in a proper LuaLClosure / GcRef.
2730	pub fn parse(
2731	    state: &mut LuaState,
2732	    dyd: DynData,
2733	    source: &[u8],
2734	    name: &[u8],
2735	    firstchar: i32,
2736	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2737	    let source_str = state.intern_str(name)?;
2738	    let envn_str = state.intern_str(lua_lex::LUA_ENV)?;
2739	
2740	    let rest_bytes: Vec<u8> = source.iter().skip(1).copied().collect();
2741	    let z = lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes(rest_bytes);
2742	
2743	    let mut lex_ls = lua_lex::LexState {
2744	        current: firstchar,
2745	        linenumber: 1,
2746	        lastline: 1,
2747	        t: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2748	        lookahead: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2749	        fs: None,
2750	        z,
2751	        buff: lua_lex::LexBuffer::new(),
2752	        h: None,
2753	        dyd: None,
2754	        source: source_str.0.clone(),
2755	        envn: envn_str.0,
2756	    };
2757	    lua_lex::next(state, &mut lex_ls)?;
2758	
2759	    let first_kind = lex_ls.t.kind;
2760	    let first_value = local_token_value(&lex_ls.t.value);
2761	
2762	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
2763	        current: lex_ls.current,
2764	        linenumber: lex_ls.linenumber,
2765	        lastline: lex_ls.lastline,
2766	        t: LexToken { token: first_kind, seminfo: first_value },
2767	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
2768	        fs: None,
2769	        dyd,
2770	        source: Some(source_str.clone()),
2771	        envn: Some(GcRef(lex_ls.envn.clone())),
2772	    };
2773	
2774	    let mut main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
2775	    main_proto.source = Some(source_str);
2776	    main_proto.is_vararg = true;
2777	    let main_fs = FuncState {
2778	        f: main_proto,
2779	        prev: None,
2780	        bl: None,
2781	        pc: 0,
2782	        lasttarget: 0,
2783	        previousline: 0,
2784	        nk: 0,
2785	        np: 0,
2786	        nabslineinfo: 0,
2787	        firstlocal: 0,
2788	        firstlabel: 0,
2789	        ndebugvars: 0,
2790	        nactvar: 0,
2791	        nups: 0,
2792	        freereg: 0,
2793	        iwthabs: 0,
2794	        needclose: false,
2795	    };
2796	
2797	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)
2798	}

2800	/// Convert a `lua_lex::TokenValue` into the local `parse::TokenValue` flat shape.
2801	///
2802	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
2803	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
2804	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {
2805	    match v {
2806	        lua_lex::TokenValue::None => TokenValue::default(),
2807	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Float(r) => TokenValue { r: *r, i: 0, ts: None },
2808	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Int(i) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: *i, ts: None },
2809	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Str(s) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: 0, ts: Some(GcRef(s.clone())) },
2810	    }
2811	}

1585	}
1586	
1587	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1588	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1589	///
1590	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1591	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1592	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1593	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1594	    let c = p.z.getc();
1595	
1596	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1597	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1598	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1599	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {
1605	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1606	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1607	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1608	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1609	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1610	    };
1611	
1612	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1613	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1614	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1615	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1616	
1617	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1618	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:
1619	    // `setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L);`). In the Rust port
1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}

content.text: Now let me check `mainfunc` more carefully. I noticed something - in `parse()`, we call `lua_lex::next(state, &mut lex_ls)` which gives us a `lex_ls.t.kind` and we transfer that as `first_kind` to the parser's `LexState`. Then we call `mainfunc`. Let me look at the parser's `LexState` more carefully — is it actually used? Let me look at what tokens are produced for `print("hello")`:

input.command: cargo run -q -p lua-cli -- 'print("hello")' 2>&1 | grep DEBUG | head -50

450	
451	// TODO_ARCH(phase-b-reconcile): re-exporting canonical OpCode from lua-code.
452	pub use lua_code::opcodes::OpCode;
453	
454	// ── Minimal LexState stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
455	// PORT NOTE: In C, LexState is defined in llex.h (→ lua-lex crate).
456	//   We declare a minimal stub here for Phase A so function bodies can be
457	//   written. Phase B will replace with `lua_lex::LexState` and remove this.
458	
459	/// Semantic info attached to a token.
460	/// C: SemInfo from llex.h; Rust analogue is TokenValue.
461	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
462	pub struct TokenValue {
463	    /// C: r — float literal value (for TK_FLT)
464	    pub r: f64,
465	    /// C: i — integer literal value (for TK_INT)
466	    pub i: i64,
467	    /// C: ts — string value (for TK_NAME, TK_STRING)
468	    pub ts: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
469	}
470	
471	/// C: Token from llex.h.
472	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
473	pub struct LexToken {
474	    pub token: TokenKind,
475	    pub seminfo: TokenValue,
476	}
477	
478	/// C: LexState — per-chunk lexer + parser state.
479	/// PORT NOTE: This is a Phase A stub. In Phase B, `LexState` lives in
480	///   `lua-lex` and `lua-parse` imports it. `FuncState` will move here
481	///   or be passed separately. The `fs` field creates a circular-crate
482	///   dependency that Phase B must resolve (likely: both live in one crate).
483	#[derive(Debug)]
484	pub struct LexState {
485	    /// C: current — current character (i32; -1 = EOZ)
486	    pub current: i32,
487	    /// C: linenumber
488	    pub linenumber: i32,
489	    /// C: lastline
490	    pub lastline: i32,
491	    /// C: t — current token
492	    pub t: LexToken,
493	    /// C: lookahead — one-token lookahead
494	    pub lookahead: LexToken,
495	    /// C: fs — current FuncState (parser owns this)

514	// ── §1 Error helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
515	
516	/// C: static l_noret error_expected(LexState *ls, int token)
517	/// Constructs a syntax error for a missing expected token.
518	/// In Rust, `l_noret` becomes returning `LuaError`; callers use
519	/// `return Err(error_expected(...))`.
520	fn error_expected(ls: &LexState, token: TokenKind) -> LuaError {
521	    // C: luaX_syntaxerror(ls, luaO_pushfstring(ls->L, "%s expected", luaX_token2str(ls, token)));
522	    // TODO(port): lua_lex::token_to_str(ls, token) for the token name
523	    LuaError::syntax(format_args!("{} expected", token))
524	}
525	
526	/// C: static l_noret errorlimit(FuncState *fs, int limit, const char *what)
527	/// Constructs a compile-time limit-exceeded syntax error.
528	fn error_limit(fs: &FuncState, limit: i32, what: &str) -> LuaError {
529	    // C: line == 0 ? "main function" : "function at line %d"
530	    let line = fs.f.linedefined;
531	    if line == 0 {
532	        LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
533	            "too many {} (limit is {}) in main function", what, limit
534	        ))
535	    } else {
536	        LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
537	            "too many {} (limit is {}) in function at line {}", what, limit, line
538	        ))
539	    }
540	}
541	
542	/// C: static void checklimit(FuncState *fs, int v, int l, const char *what)
543	fn check_limit(fs: &FuncState, v: i32, l: i32, what: &str) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
544	    if v > l {
545	        return Err(error_limit(fs, l, what));
546	    }
547	    Ok(())
548	}
549

552	/// C: static int testnext(LexState *ls, int c)
553	/// If the current token matches `c`, consume it and return true.
554	fn test_next(ls: &mut LexState, state: &mut LuaState, c: TokenKind) -> Result<bool, LuaError> {
555	    if ls.t.token == c {
556	        // C: luaX_next(ls)
557	        // TODO(port): lua_lex::next(ls, state)?;
558	        Ok(true)
559	    } else {
560	        Ok(false)
561	    }
562	}
563	
564	/// C: static void check(LexState *ls, int c)
565	fn check(ls: &LexState, c: TokenKind) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
566	    if ls.t.token != c {
567	        return Err(error_expected(ls, c));
568	    }
569	    Ok(())
570	}
571	
572	/// C: static void checknext(LexState *ls, int c)
573	fn check_next(ls: &mut LexState, state: &mut LuaState, c: TokenKind) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
574	    check(ls, c)?;
575	    // C: luaX_next(ls)
576	    // TODO(port): lua_lex::next(ls, state)?;
577	    Ok(())
578	}

355	// C: typedef struct LexState { ... } LexState;
356	// types.tsv: LexState → LexState;  LexState.L removed (thread via &mut LuaState)
357	/// Per-chunk lexer (and shared parser) state.
358	///
359	/// Corresponds to `LexState` in `llex.h`.  Owns the input stream, token
360	/// buffer, and current/lookahead tokens.
361	///
362	/// # C mapping (types.tsv)
363	/// ```text
364	/// LexState.current    → current: i32        (charint; -1 = EOZ)
365	/// LexState.linenumber → linenumber: i32
366	/// LexState.lastline   → lastline: i32
367	/// LexState.t          → t: Token            (current token)
368	/// LexState.lookahead  → lookahead: Token    (one-token lookahead)
369	/// LexState.fs         → fs: Option<Box<FuncState>>   (parser state)
370	/// LexState.L          → (removed; callers pass &mut LuaState)
371	/// LexState.z          → z: ZIO              (owned input stream)
372	/// LexState.buff       → buff: LexBuffer     (owned token-text buffer)
373	/// LexState.h          → h: GcRef<LuaTable>  (string-anchor table)
374	/// LexState.dyd        → dyd: DynData        (parser dynamic data)
375	/// LexState.source     → source: GcRef<LuaString>
376	/// LexState.envn       → envn: GcRef<LuaString>
377	/// ```
378	pub struct LexState {
379	    // C: int current;  /* current character (charint) */
380	    pub current: i32,
381	    // C: int linenumber;  /* input line counter */
382	    pub linenumber: i32,
383	    // C: int lastline;  /* line of last token 'consumed' */
384	    pub lastline: i32,
385	    // C: Token t;  /* current token */
386	    pub t: Token,
387	    // C: Token lookahead;  /* look ahead token */
388	    pub lookahead: Token,
389	    // C: struct FuncState *fs;  /* current function (parser) */
390	    // TODO(port): Box<FuncState> once FuncState lands in lua-parse (Phase B)
391	    pub fs: Option<()>,
392	    // C: ZIO *z;  /* input stream */
393	    // PORT NOTE: C held a pointer; Rust owns the ZIO directly per types.tsv.
394	    pub z: ZIO,
395	    // C: Mbuffer *buff;  /* buffer for tokens */
396	    // PORT NOTE: C held a pointer; Rust owns the LexBuffer directly per types.tsv.
397	    pub buff: LexBuffer,
398	    // C: Table *h;  /* to avoid collection/reuse strings */
399	    // TODO(port): GcRef<LuaTable> once LuaTable is defined in Phase B
400	    pub h: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
401	    // C: struct Dyndata *dyd;  /* dynamic structures used by the parser */
402	    // TODO(port): DynData once parser types land in Phase B
403	    pub dyd: Option<()>,
404	    // C: TString *source;  /* current source name */
405	    pub source: GcRef<LuaString>,
406	    // C: TString *envn;  /* environment variable name */
407	    pub envn: GcRef<LuaString>,
408	}
